function.name: submit_work
function.arguments: {"technology_choices": [{"category": "web_framework", "choice": "Flask", "rationale": "Existing Python Flask API \u2014 extend rather than replace"}, {"category": "frontend", "choice": "Vanilla JavaScript", "rationale": "Simple fetch-based UI with no framework dependency"}], "component_boundaries": [{"name": "api", "responsibility": "REST API serving JSON endpoints via Flask", "dependencies": []}, {"name": "ui", "responsibility": "Browser frontend fetching from API and rendering responses", "dependencies": ["api"]}], "data_flow": "Browser (ui/app.js) sends GET requests to Flask API (api/app.py). API returns JSON responses with message fields. No database or external services involved.", "decisions": [{"id": "ARCH-001", "title": "Extend existing Flask app", "decision": "Add /goodbye route to existing api/app.py rather than creating a new service", "rationale": "Single-file API with simple routing \u2014 microservice split would add unnecessary complexity"}], "actors": [{"name": "End User", "type": "human", "triggers": ["sends HTTP request to API"]}], "integrations": [{"name": "Flask API", "direction": "inbound", "protocol": "http"}], "test_surface": {"integration_flows": [{"name": "primary-api-flow", "components_involved": ["api"], "description": "Validates the primary integration surface for this plan's architecture"}], "e2e_flows": [{"actor": "End User", "steps": ["exercise the feature"], "success_criteria": ["response is valid"]}]}}
